hero_tagline: Planning your family getaway to the holiday parks around Denmark means discovering which one feels just right for your crew, whether you want beachside adventures or peaceful forest retreats.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Denmark
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Denmark? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Denmark so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Denmark is a lush, green pocket of WA's south coast where BIG4 Ocean Beach's massive playground and regular kangaroo visitors make it a genuine standout for young families.
local_knowledge: BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach is the clear pick, with a massive playground featuring a flying fox and bouncy pillows, plus regular kangaroo encounters right around the park, and inlet swimming across the road. The other parks in town lean more toward nature-based, unstructured activities, river kayaking, fairy trails and visiting chickens, better suited to families happy with a slower pace. Ocean Beach itself, along with the Wilson Inlet, offers a good choice between surf and calm water swimming. Greens Pool and Elephant Rocks, a short drive towards William Bay National Park, are among WA's most photographed swimming spots, with calm, crystal-clear water protected by granite boulders.

if_we_were_booking.label: Playground and wildlife
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach for the massive playground with a flying fox and bouncy pillows, plus regular kangaroo sightings and inlet swimming.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Drive to Greens Pool in William Bay National Park, the calm, crystal-clear water protected by granite boulders is genuinely one of the best family swimming spots in the country, and it's completely free.

faq.question: Is Denmark good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach has excellent facilities, and nearby Greens Pool is among the best family swimming spots in WA.

faq.question: Which Denmark park is best for kids?
faq.answer: BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach, with a massive playground featuring a flying fox and bouncy pillows, plus regular kangaroo encounters.

faq.question: What is Greens Pool?
faq.answer: A naturally sheltered swimming spot in William Bay National Park, with calm, crystal-clear water protected by granite boulders, a short drive from Denmark.

faq.question: Will kids see kangaroos in Denmark?
faq.answer: Sightings around BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach are common, though as with all wildlife, not guaranteed on every visit.

faq.question: Is the inlet or ocean better for young children?
faq.answer: The Wilson Inlet offers calmer water than the ocean beaches, generally better suited to younger swimmers.

faq.question: What is Elephant Rocks?
faq.answer: A striking rock formation near Greens Pool in William Bay National Park, another excellent, sheltered family swimming spot.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Greens Pool and Elephant Rocks, both free to visit and genuinely spectacular.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Spring through autumn for the mildest weather, though the region can be cooler than other WA coastal destinations year-round.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: BIG4 Denmark Ocean Beach's covered play areas offer some shelter, and the town's cafes provide a relaxed indoor option.

faq.question: How far is Denmark from Albany?
faq.answer: About 30 to 40 minutes drive, making it easy to combine both destinations in one trip.
